description: You are an art historian at the Museum of Modern Art preparing catalogue notes for a major Van Gogh retrospective. Verify the astronomical interpretation of 'The Starry Night' by observing the pre-dawn sky from Saint-Rémy-de-Provence, France (43.789°N, 4.831°E, 60m) on June 19, 1889, at 04:30 UTC. Capture screenshots of Venus, the star Aldebaran, and a wide view of the eastern sky showing constellations to ~/Pictures/stellarium/. Document your findings in a file at ~/Desktop/starry_night_analysis.txt, including the date, location, Venus visibility, nearby constellations, and your assessment of the painting's accuracy. The analysis requires a view with atmosphere, constellation lines, and planet labels enabled, and the ground hidden for an unobstructed view.
